history.text: Help me prioritize my renovation projects

To prioritize your renovation projects effectively, start by categorizing them based on safety and necessity. Address any structural issues, water damage, or electrical hazards immediately, as these pose risks if ignored. Next, consider projects that affect your daily functionality or comfort, such as fixing a leaky roof or updating inefficient heating systems, especially if they impact your ability to live comfortably in the home. After covering essentials, evaluate projects based on your available budget and timeline; smaller tasks like painting or updating lighting can often be done in parallel with larger structural works. Finally, think about the return on investment and personal goals, such as increasing property value or preparing the home for a sale. Create a simple list ranking these items from most urgent to least, and schedule them according to your financial resources and seasonal constraints. This approach ensures you tackle the most critical needs first while balancing cost and personal preference.
